div.h1: ECS: Spawn and Get ID Challenge
div.p: Problem: Entity commands are typically deferred to the end of the frame to avoid structural invalidation during system iteration. However, the calling system often needs the Entity ID immediately to link other entities or components in the same frame.
Solution: Split ID allocation (Immediate) from component storage insertion (Deferred).

text.tspan: // 1. We need this ID right now!
// 2. To use it as a reference here:
// If 'spawn' is fully deferred, 'bullet' would be null/future.
// 1. We need this ID right now!
// 2. To use it as a reference here:
// If 'spawn' is fully deferred, 'bullet' would be null/future.

g.text: In a fully deferred model, the ID isn't known until the Command Buffer flushes.

text.tspan: // No-op: The ID exists. We just ensure
// metadata entries exist in internal maps.
// No-op: The ID exists. We just ensure
// metadata entries exist in internal maps.
// ID is already allocated by EntityManager
// ID is already allocated by EntityManager

p.em: Can be passed by value, copied to registers.

ul.li: Result: A valid Entity handle is returned to the user now.
State: ID is unique but has no components attached yet.
Command::Spawn and Command::Insert are pushed to a Vec<Command>.
Components are stored in type-erased Box<dyn Any>.
The allocated ID is embedded in these command objects.
The World processes the buffer exclusively.
